title: Centre for New Economy and Society - Home
gist: This is a WEF Centre for the New Economy and Society landing page — mostly mission/marketing boilerplate — but it discloses concrete scale metrics: 180+ business partners, a Reskilling Revolution reaching 350M+ people (target 1 billion), and gender-parity work across 14 countries benefiting 850,000+ women.

insights.claim: The World Economic Forum's Reskilling Revolution platform has reached more than 350 million people worldwide since its January 2020 launch, against a stated goal of preparing 1 billion people for tomorrow's economy.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: A concrete measure of the WEF's convening scale in workforce reskilling; useful benchmark for edtech and corporate skilling initiatives tracking global upskilling momentum.
insights.quote: More than 350 million people around the world are being reached by the Forum's Reskilling Revolution platform since it was launched in January 2020.
insights.locator: Preparing 1 billion people for tomorrow's economy

insights.claim: The WEF Centre for the New Economy and Society reports a partner network of 180+ global business partners, 100+ academic institutions/civil society/international organizations, and 45+ partner governments.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
entities: Centre for the New Economy and Society
insights.so_what: Signals the institutional weight behind WEF economy-and-society agendas — relevant for gauging which public-private standards may gain adoption traction.
insights.quote: 180+ Global business partners100+Academic institutions, civil society, and international  organizations45+Partner governments

insights.claim: The WEF has supported 14 countries to convene Gender Parity Accelerators, work it credits with benefiting over 850,000 women by closing economic gender gaps in the private sector.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Quantifies WEF's gender-parity footprint; a marker for investors tracking diversity/ESG programs and their claimed reach.
insights.quote: The World Economic Forum has supported 14 countries to convene Gender Parity Accelerators that are helping close economic gender gaps.
insights.locator: Closing gender gaps in the private sector

insights.claim: The WEF frames its economy-and-society work around three pillars — fostering economic growth and risk preparedness, investing in talent and human capital, and promoting equity and inclusion.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
entities: Centre for the New Economy and Society
insights.so_what: Maps the thematic buckets WEF will fund and publish on, hinting where its future standards, reports, and convening efforts will concentrate.
insights.quote: boosting economic growth while preparing for potential risks, promoting equity and inclusion, and investing in talent and human capital
insights.locator: We channel our efforts into three critical areas

insights.claim: The WEF's Future of Growth Initiative explicitly frames a shift 'from the growth we have to the growth we need,' signaling an institutional push to redefine growth quality rather than pursue raw GDP expansion.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Reflects an elite-consensus tilt toward 'quality of growth' metrics beyond GDP — relevant for anticipating policy and ESG framework direction.
insights.quote: Promoting and supporting the transition from the growth we have to the growth we need.

insights.claim: The WEF's Global Parity Sprint 2030 sets an explicit target of accelerating gender parity in economic participation and leadership by 2030.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: A dated benchmark (2030) against which WEF gender-parity progress can be measured and held accountable.
insights.quote: Accelerating gender parity in economic participation and leadership by 2030.

top_entities: Centre for the New Economy and Society
